Newest Records

Newest folklore, legend, and mystery stories

The latest Kyunolab Mystery Archive entries, including urban legends, internet folklore, myths, strange places, and source-aware mystery notes.

Modern Legends

Goatman Modern Legend: Bridges, Backroads, and the Half-Human Figure

Goatman is a modern monster legend about a half-human, half-goat figure often connected with bridges, wooded roads, and local warnings.

Classic Folklore

Headless Horseman Legend: Night Roads, Missing Heads, and the Rider in Folklore

The Headless Horseman is a ghostly rider figure remembered through Sleepy Hollow and older folklore about headless apparitions on roads or battle-haunted places.

Classic Folklore - Rider Folklore - 9 min read - Updated Jul 20, 2026
Unexplained Mysteries

Flying Dutchman Folklore: The Ghost Ship, the Horizon, and a Maritime Legend

The Flying Dutchman is a maritime legend about a ghost ship condemned to sail the seas and appear on the horizon.

Unexplained Mysteries - Ghost Ship Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 20, 2026
Strange Places

Tower of London Ghosts: Royal Memory, Execution Stories, and a Haunted Landmark

Tower of London ghost stories attach haunting traditions to a real historic landmark associated with imprisonment, executions, royal power, and public memory.

Internet Folklore

Jeff the Killer Creepypasta: The Smile, the Image, and an Internet Horror Legend

Jeff the Killer is a creepypasta figure known for a pale face, carved-looking smile, and the phrase go to sleep.

Urban Legends

The Licked Hand Urban Legend: The Dog, the Bed, and the Message on the Wall

The Licked Hand is a modern urban legend about someone alone at night who feels a reassuring lick from what they believe is the family dog.

Urban Legends - Bedroom Urban Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 20, 2026
Legend Origins

Evil Eye Origin: How a Harmful Glance Became One of the World's Oldest Protective Beliefs

The evil eye is the belief that a harmful or envious glance can bring misfortune. Many cultures developed charms, gestures, or sayings to protect against it.

Legend Origins - Protective Belief Origin - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Mythic Objects

Sword in the Stone Legend: The Arthurian Object That Proved a King

The Sword in the Stone is an Arthurian legend in which the rightful king is revealed by pulling a sword from stone or anvil. The act proves legitimacy through an impossible test.

Mythic Objects - Arthurian Object - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Legendary Places

Mount Kailash Legendary Place: The Sacred Mountain People Circle But Do Not Climb

Mount Kailash is a sacred mountain in Tibet revered in several religious traditions. It is known for pilgrimage circuits around the mountain rather than ordinary summit climbing.

Legendary Places - Sacred Mountain - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Strange Nature

Raining Animals Phenomenon: When Fish, Frogs, and Weather Become Folklore

Raining animals refers to reports of fish, frogs, or other small creatures falling from the sky. Possible explanations often involve waterspouts or strong weather lifting animals from nearby water.

Strange Nature - Unusual Weather Phenomenon - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Lost Worlds

Lyonesse Lost Land Legend: The Drowned Kingdom at the Edge of Arthurian Memory

Lyonesse is a legendary lost land often placed off the coast of Cornwall. Later tradition connects it with drowned kingdoms, coastal memory, and Arthurian material.

Lost Worlds - Drowned Land Legend - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
Mythic Creatures

Griffin Folklore: The Eagle-Lion Guardian of Treasure and Power

The griffin is a mythic creature usually combining the head and wings of an eagle with the body of a lion. It is often linked with guardianship, treasure, and powerful places.

Mythic Creatures - Hybrid Guardian Creature - 9 min read - Updated Jul 19, 2026
